codeReview.summary: The feature adds the requested read operations and OTEL spans, but `getBranch()` is not correct: it converts every `GitCommandFailed` into the detached-HEAD sentinel `"HEAD"` instead of only handling the specific detached-HEAD git error. That means genuine failures such as a non-repository path or other git command errors are silently misreported as success.

issues.description: `getBranch()` catches every `GitCommandFailed` from `git symbolic-ref --short HEAD` and returns `HEAD`. This masks real failures (for example, a non-git directory or other git errors) as detached HEAD, violating the expected behavior that only detached HEAD should yield `HEAD`.
The tests cover the detached-HEAD case but do not cover non-detached failures for `getBranch()`, so the implementation bug above is not caught by the suite.

summary: Reviewed the feature handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `818c237`. The package implements the requested read operations and tracing, but the review fails because `getBranch()` treats every git command failure as detached HEAD and can therefore return a false-positive `HEAD` instead of surfacing real errors.
